title: Functional gameplay shift: exploration geometry, field requests, and field-station upgrades
summary: Twenty-first structured packet for Eco Explorer. The user wants the game to feel more game-like after the current living-world stopping point. The scout lane has now grounded the first two follow-ons: `main-51` should be a forest-first traversal proof centered in late `fern-hollow` into `log-run`, using an upper log route plus one short sheltered lower lane rather than a true cave-engine rewrite; `main-53` should be a world-map-context field-station ledger that converts surveyed biomes plus completed notebook requests into field credit for one gentle movement upgrade. Sketchbook, broader sound, and close-look inspect remain approved, but they should now sit behind this gameplay lane.

execution_order.goal: Turn the user's more-game-like direction into one grounded sequence for exploration, requests, and upgrades.
execution_order.why_now: The project now has enough educational and atmospheric scaffolding that the next sharp gain is a stronger play loop, not another narrow polish system.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a compact exploration-geometry handoff with one best biome candidate for a shallow cave, hollow, or underpass proof.
execution_order.why_now: Verticality and a short down-and-back detour are the safest first way to make the world feel more playable without changing genres.

execution_order.goal: Implement one richer traversal proof with more vertical routes and one short cave or hollow detour.
execution_order.why_now: Movement and terrain should get stronger before the game adds another meta-layer on top.

execution_order.goal: Review the exploration proof for readability, flow, difficulty, and whether it still feels cozy.
execution_order.why_now: Traversal changes can quietly turn into friction or confusion if they are not reviewed against the live child-facing feel.

execution_order.goal: Add a tiny notebook-style field-request layer tied to exploration and ecology patterns.
execution_order.why_now: Once terrain can reward curiosity, the next layer should add purpose without becoming a quest log.

execution_order.goal: Review the first field-request pass for tone, clutter, and science grounding.
execution_order.why_now: Requests are useful only if they still feel like naturalist prompts instead of chores.

execution_order.goal: Ground the upgrade economy around a field-station or research-cart model instead of direct specimen selling.
execution_order.why_now: The user wants a market/power-up loop, but the science tone is better served by a survey-token or field-credit model.

execution_order.goal: Add one tiny field-station upgrade loop with one gentle movement upgrade.
execution_order.why_now: A modest upgrade loop can make exploration feel more game-like without swamping the educational core.

execution_order.goal: Review the field-station loop for ethics, pacing, and cozy fit.
execution_order.why_now: The economy framing matters a lot for a nature game aimed at kids, so this needs an explicit science-safe review.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate.
Keep the game cozy, exploratory, and child-readable; do not turn it into combat, grind, or precision platforming.
Use the current `256x160` viewport baseline and keep any new HUD or objective surface compact.
Requests should feel like notebook prompts or field assignments, not like chores, timers, or achievement grinds.
The first request pass should reuse existing journal, survey, prompt, and world-state evidence instead of introducing a separate quest-log state model.
Avoid a literal market that teaches direct selling of living nature finds.
If an economy exists, route it through survey progress, completed requests, field credit, stamps, or a research-station model instead of extraction-first selling.
Collectibles should stay discovery flavor and ecology evidence in v1, not direct currency.
The first upgrade should be modest, legible, and movement-oriented, such as slightly faster walk speed or a small jump boost.
Caves and vertical routes should be short authored detours, not long dungeon spaces.
The first forest traversal proof should stay in late `fern-hollow` into `log-run` so start, map-return, and corridor anchors remain clear.
The first traversal proof should prefer a sheltered lower lane such as a root-hollow or hollow-log underpass instead of a generic overhang cave system.
Exploration geometry should stay forgiving and readable for kids ages 7-10.
acceptance_focus: The long-term plan now favors a stronger play loop over narrower polish features.
Future game-like systems stay science-safe and cozy instead of drifting into generic achievement design.
Exploration, objectives, and upgrades all build on the current journal and ecology systems rather than replacing them.
Field requests stay notebook-first and evidence-aware instead of opening a separate contract or quest-log surface.
Upgrade rewards convert fieldwork into gentle field credit rather than pricing individual finds.
The first traversal proof strengthens forest exploration without reopening a large cave or tilemap system.
The first station loop stays world-map-context and ledger-light instead of bloating the in-biome field menu.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-functional-gameplay-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-functional-gameplay-loop-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-forest-traversal-proof-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-field-station-credit-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-world-continuity-preproduction-pack.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-continuous-corridor-travel-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-depth-and-game-feel-sequence.md, and this packet. Treat the next strategic shift as functional gameplay: first a forest-only traversal proof in late `fern-hollow` into `log-run` using a sheltered lower lane instead of a true cave-engine rewrite, then notebook-style field requests built from journal, survey, and world-state evidence, then a world-map-context field-station ledger built on field credit rather than specimen selling. Keep sketchbook, broader sound, and close-look mode approved but lower priority than this lane.
